name: vscode-internals
description: Expose the full vscode.* API as a token-protected local REST + SSE service with dynamic OpenAPI. Spiritual successor to vs-rest-api.

commands.title: VSCode Internals: Copy Token to Clipboard
VSCode Internals: Open API Docs (Swagger UI)

vscodeInternals.port.description: Preferred port the local HTTP server binds to. If the port is already in use (e.g. another VSCode window already has the extension active) and `portAutoIncrement` is enabled, the server tries `port + 1`, `port + 2`, … in turn. Restart required.

vscodeInternals.portAutoIncrement.description: On port collision (EADDRINUSE), try the next port up to `portAutoIncrementMax` times before failing. Lets multiple VSCode windows coexist without manual port assignment. Disable to enforce a strict port — the server will refuse to start instead of bumping.

vscodeInternals.portAutoIncrementMax.description: Maximum number of port-bump attempts when `portAutoIncrement` is enabled. 0 means no bumping (equivalent to `portAutoIncrement: false`).

vscodeInternals.host.description: Bind address. Default 127.0.0.1 (loopback only). Set to 0.0.0.0 only if you understand the security implications — anyone with network access to this machine could drive your editor with a leaked token.

vscodeInternals.autoStart.description: Start the server automatically on extension activation.

vscodeInternals.maxBodySizeBytes.description: Maximum request body size in bytes (default 10 MiB). Increase if you intend to send large file contents.

vscodeInternals.logLevel.description: Log verbosity in the VSCode Internals output channel.

vscodeInternals.showStatusBar.description: Show the VSCode Internals item in the status bar. Disable to hide it; the server runs regardless. Reload required.

vscodeInternals.showStartupNotifications.description: Show informational toasts at server start: the port-bump notification (when the configured port was taken) and the non-loopback host warning. Disable to silence both. Errors are always shown.

vscodeInternals.devMode.markdownDescription: ⚠️ **Dangerous.** Enables `/dev/eval` (arbitrary code execution), `/dev/info`, the `vscodeInternals.restart` command, and writes a token-handshake temp file. Anyone with the token can run arbitrary code as your user. Reload required.
